exercises.title: OPERATOR GREETING
exercises.prompt:
Define a method **greet(handle)** that returns a greeting string.

Given a handle, return exactly:
~~~text
Welcome to the Construct, <handle>.
~~~
Use string interpolation: **#{handle}**.

~~~ruby
greet("Trinity")  # "Welcome to the Construct, Trinity."
~~~
exercises.starter:
def greet(handle)
  # TODO: interpolate the handle
  ""
end
exercises.lore: Every jack-in starts with a handshake.

exercises.title: ID TAG
exercises.prompt:
Define a method **id_tag(name, level)** that returns a badge string combining a name and a numeric level.

Return exactly:
~~~text
[<name>] clearance level <level>
~~~
Both values go inside one interpolated string.

~~~ruby
id_tag("Switch", 7)  # "[Switch] clearance level 7"
~~~
exercises.starter:
def id_tag(name, level)
  # TODO: combine name and level
  name
end
exercises.hint: Two #{} slots in one string: "[#{name}] clearance level #{level}".

exercises.title: PING SWEEP
exercises.prompt:
Define a method **ping(host)** that **puts** (prints) the line:
~~~text
PING <host> ... ACK
~~~
Then, at the top level, call it three times with **"core"**, **"relay"**, and **"gateway"**, in that order.

~~~ruby
ping("core")    # prints: PING core ... ACK
~~~
exercises.starter:
def ping(host)
  # TODO: puts the ping line
end

# TODO: call ping three times
exercises.solution:
def ping(host)
  puts "PING #{host} ... ACK"
end

ping("core")
ping("relay")
ping("gateway")

tests.name: something was printed
tests.code: assert(stdout().length > 0, "Nothing printed. Use puts inside ping.")

tests.name: all three hosts acked
tests.code:
o = stdout()
assert(o.include?("PING core ... ACK"), "missing core")
assert(o.include?("PING relay ... ACK"), "missing relay")
assert(o.include?("PING gateway ... ACK"), "missing gateway")

exercises.hint: Inside ping: puts "PING #{host} ... ACK". Then call it three times.
exercises.lore: Listen for the echo in the dark.

exercises.title: STATUS LINE
exercises.prompt:
Define a method **status_line(name, online)** that returns a status string. The **online** argument is a boolean.

When **online** is true return:
~~~text
<name>: ONLINE
~~~
When it is false return:
~~~text
<name>: OFFLINE
~~~
The state word changes; the name is interpolated.

~~~ruby
status_line("MAGI", true)   # "MAGI: ONLINE"
status_line("MAGI", false)  # "MAGI: OFFLINE"
~~~
exercises.starter:
def status_line(name, online)
  # TODO: pick ONLINE or OFFLINE
  "#{name}: ONLINE"
end
exercises.solution:
def status_line(name, online)
  state = online ? "ONLINE" : "OFFLINE"
  "#{name}: #{state}"
end

tests.name: both branches differ
tests.code: assert(status_line("X", true) != status_line("X", false), "the state must change with the flag")

exercises.lore: A green light is a promise the grid keeps.

exercises.title: BOOT BANNER
exercises.prompt:
Define a method **banner(title)** that returns a TWO-LINE string: the title, then a line of dashes (**-**) exactly as long as the title. Separate the two lines with a newline **\n**.

~~~ruby
banner("CORE")
# "CORE\n----"
~~~
Use interpolation and **String#length**. The string **"-" * n** repeats a dash n times.
exercises.starter:
def banner(title)
  # TODO: title on line 1, matching dashes on line 2
  title
end
exercises.hint: "#{title}\n#{"-" * title.length}" gives a rule the same width as the title.
exercises.lore: Every system announces itself before it thinks.
